title: Cochlear Implant | MedlinePlus
content: Cochlear Implants On this page Basics Summary Start Here Learn More Related Issues See, Play and Learn Videos and Tutorials Research Statistics and Research Journal Articles Resources Find an Expert For You Children Patient Handouts Summary A cochlear implant is a small, complex electronic device that can help to provide a sense of sound. People who are profoundly deaf or severely hard-of-hearing can get help from them. The implant consists of two parts. One part sits on the outside of the body, behind the ear. A second part is surgically placed under the skin. An implant does not restore normal hearing. It can help a person understand speech. Children and adults can benefit from them.
